title: Paraixeris humifusa
intro: Paraixeris humifusa, also known as Korean wormwood, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Korea and Japan. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Paraixeris humifusa include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional Korean medicine, known as "hanbang," to treat a range of health issues.
taxonomy: Paraixeris humifusa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Paraixeris, which is a part of the tribe Senecioneae. Paraixeris humifusa is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and the Korean Peninsula. The plant grows as a low-growing, spreading herb with small, yellow or white flowers. It is closely related to other members of the Senecioneae tribe, which includes various species of thistles and similar plants.
